title: What Card Should You Apply for Next?
author: Navees
summary: I get this question all the time. If you've got a 700+ score and a couple of cards, here's the exact order I'd apply next.
body_html:
I get this question all the time. So please read.

Let's assume you have a couple of cards and a credit score over 700+. Now you can look into churning.

Step 1: Make sure you're under 5/24. That means approved for under 5 cards in the past 24 months. (How to check? Go into Credit Karma and see the card open date for every card you have.)

Step 2: Decide if you want Travel or Statement Credit.

Step 3: If you decide travel, think about which airline or air alliance.

Only then do you look at the cards. Otherwise it's just overwhelming with the amount of cards available.

Some ideal* examples for travel: if you're living in Denver or Houston, think United Explorer. If you're in Dallas or Charlotte, think AA card. Ideal means it's nice to have — I've had a lot of luck with all 3 alliances.

The next few years, mapped out

Say you start your churning journey now. Here's what the next few years could look like (assuming SUBs and fees stay roughly similar and you value travel over statement credit). I've updated this for where the offers sit right now:

DateCardIssuerSUBAFx/24
2026 AugSapphire PreferredChase100K UR$951/24
2026 NovInk Business CashChase100K UR$01/24*
2027 FebUnited ExplorerChase60K miles$952/24
2027 JunAAdvantage Platinum SelectCiti60K miles$993/24
2027 OctVenture RewardsCapital One75K miles$954/24
2028 FebIHG Rewards PremierChase185K + FN$995/24
2028 JunWorld of HyattChase60K + FN$955/24
2028 OctAmex PlatinumAmex175K+ MR$6955/24

*Business cards like the Ink don't add to your 5/24 count with Chase, so they're free slots — that's why I slot one in early. It's not hard to open a sole-trader/LLC just for the sign-up bonus; it's cheap and quick, and as long as you stick to miles-and-points SUBs (not cash), you're fine.

As you can see — we hit repeated bonuses. THIS is the way to maximise rewards.
